def: see s.v. انتہ antah.

s.m. Interior, inside, contents, the midst; soul, mind, heart, supreme soul; mystery, secret; property, peculiarity; difference, contrast ( = farq); hole, opening, gap, interval, intermediate space or time ( = biććhā and fāṣila); distance; corner, side, quarter of the heavens; period, term; pause, stop; opportunity, occasion; — adj. Interior, internal, inner; secret, hidden; intermediate, interposed; intermittent; distant; different, other; similar; near, related, intimate; — adv. On one side, out of the way, apart, separate; except, without; in the middle or interior; in, within, between, amongst ( = S. antar)
subs: (S. antara+apatya), adj. Pregnant (a woman); with young (an animal)
s.m. The soul, the inherent supreme spirit
s.m. Internal or inherent nature, disposition; — adj. Inherent, innate, inbred
part. Situated in the midst; being within
antar-bedī, s.f. The region of Kannauj lying between the Ganges and the Jamnā, commonly called the Doʼāb; part of a country lying between two rivers (as Mesopotamia)
s.m. Curtain or screen placed between the bride and bridegroom until the right moment of union is arrived; curtain, screen
(S. antar+yāmī), s.m. Lit. 'Pervading the inward parts'; the Supreme spirit, Brahmā, Vishnu
s.m. Knower of the inward parts or of the heart, the Deity
s.m. A private or secret door (within the house)
s.m. Disappearance, invisibility, vanishing out of sight; concealment; profound meditation ( = antar-dhyān); — adj. Invisible, out of sight; concealed
To disappear, become invisible; to vanish; to be concealed

adj. Gone into, entered; being in the interior, internal, innermost, hidden, secret; — adv. In the interior (of -ke), within, inside; — s.f. = antar-gati, s.f. Inward sensations or feelings, emotions; what is in the mind, mental state; — adj. Vanished from the mind, forgotten; — antar-gangā, s.m. A kind of water plant bearing a white flower, Pistia stratiotes

def: adv. In the midst, in the interior, inside, within, among, between; during, in the interval between morning and evening; in the meantime; nearly, almost; near at hand; therein; except, without

adv. Elsewhere, to another place. (See anat.)
adv. = ante, q.v.

def: s.f. A reel or frame on which thread, &c. is wound ( = aṭeran, q.v.); a hank or bundle of thread; &c. ( = āṅṭī, q.v.); — the space or junction between two fingers; two fingers placed one upon the other; — stealing or pilfering money, &c. by concealing it between two fingers

To wind thread on the hand, to make hanks of thread
anṭī mārnā, To steal money by slipping it between two fingers; to conceal anything between two fingers; to pilfer.

def: adj. Near, proximate.

wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀸 (appā), from Sanskrit आत्मन् (ātmán, “soul, spirit; self”), from Proto-Indo-Aryan *HáHtmā, from Proto-Indo-Iranian *HáHtmā, from Proto-Indo-European *h₁éh₁t-mō ~ *h₁h₁t-m̥nés (“breath, spirit”), from *h₁eh₁t- (“to breathe”) + *-mō. Cognate with Gujarati આપું (āpũ), Nepali आंत (ānta), Marathi आपण (āpaṇ), Bengali আপনি (apni), Sinhalese අපි (api). Compare Kamkata-viri amú (…

def: s.m. Selfishness, egotism. — āp-āp karnā, v.t. To address (one) with respect or deference; to flatter; to be selfish or egotistical. — āp-bhātī, adv. According to one's own pleasure. — āp-bītī, s.f. That which has befallen oneself, the story of one's own sufferings. — āp-rūp, adj. Self-made, self-formed; singular, strange, wonderful, unique, incomparable, unequalled
subs: the Deity; — you, your honour, your worship; — an unparalleled scamp or rogue. — āp-rūpī, adj. Self-formed, &c. ( = āp-rūp)

spontaneously. — āp-swārthī, adj. Pursuing one's own objects, self-seeking, selfish (see swārthī). — āp-se, āp-se-āp, adv. Of himself, of his own accord, voluntarily, freely; without rhyme or reason, causelessly; of itself, spontaneously. — āp-kāj (S. ātma-kārya), s.m. One's own business or affairs. — āp-kājī, adj. Attending to, or concerned about one's own business or affairs; pursuing one's own objects, self-seeking, selfish. — āp-ko (or apne-ko) dūr khainćnā (-se), To hold oneself aloof (from); to have an inordinate opinion of oneself. — āp-hī-āp, adv. Of himself, of itself, &c. ( = āp-se-āp); alone.

wety: Borrowed from Chagatai اپا (apa, “older sister”), not to be confused with the term of Prakrit origin, perhaps a feminine derivative of, 𑀅𑀧𑁆𑀧 (appa, “father”); see Hindi आपा (āpā).

def: An inseparable Sanskrit prefix (the reverse of अधि adhi), denoting loss, privation, negation, separation, detraction, contrariety, inferiority, badness, &c.

s.f. Self, yourself, themselves; each for him or herself; of himself, &c. ( = āp-se-āp). — āpā bisrānā, v.n. To forget, or lose consciousness of, self; to put aside self; to mortify the body. — āpā tajnā, v.n. To give up self; to sacrifice self; to give oneself up to grief, sorrow, suffering, &c.; to cast off self (i.e. the body), to commit suicide. — āpā-dhāpī, s.f. A race for self, eagerness to gratify self, selfishness. — āpā-dhāpī paṛnā, v.n. To pursue, or be intent on, the gratification of self, or the advancing of one's own interests. — āpam-dhāp = āpā-dhāp. — āpe-se bāhar honā, To be beside oneself (with joy or anger), to be in ecstacies; to be in a fury; to lose all control over self. — āpe-meṅ ānā, To come to oneself; to come to one's senses, to recover from a swoon, or from insensibility, or from a fit of anger, or from intoxication, or from fatigue.
wety: Borrowed from Chagatai اپا (apa, “older sister”), not to be confused with the term of Prakrit origin, perhaps a feminine derivative of, 𑀅𑀧𑁆𑀧 (appa, “father”); see Hindi आपा (āpā).

def: = اپواد apavād, q.v.
= اپوادي apavādī, q.v.
= اپوش apavaś, q.v.
s.m. Common or vulgar language, ungrammatical speech.
s.f. Low, vulgar, or obscene language.
s.m. Fear; fear of oneself.
s.m. Common or vulgar language, ungrammatical speech.
= اپوتر apavitra, q.v.
= اپوترتا apavitratā, q.v.
= اپکش apaksh, q.v.
s.m. Loss, detriment, waste.
s.f. = آپد āpad. — apadā-yukta, adj. (f. -ā). Beset with troubles or evils; dangerous, risky.
s.m. Taking away; (in Gram.) the ablative case.
For compounds having āpā for the first member, v.s.v. آپا āpā.
s.f. Ill-behaviour; misdemeanour; ill-repute; evil plight, forlorn condition.

ety: S. अ+पाद+हस्त, but the Skr. order is hasta-pāda
def: s.m. A cripple; a lazy, indolent person; a person who never visits anyone. (Written also apāhij, and apāj.)
s.m. (f. -ikā), = S اپہاري अपहारी apahārī, s.m. (f. -inī), Plunderer, robber, thief.
s.m. Carrying off, plundering, robbing, stealing.
s.m. (f. -inī), = S اپہارك अपहारक apahārak, s.m. (f. -ikā), Plunderer, robber, thief.
v.t. To carry off, plunder, steal, &c.
s.m. (f. -trī), Robber, plunderer, thief.
s.m. Denial; concealment.
v.n. To come up to; to arrive.
s.m. Disgrace, dishonour, ignominy, infamy.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Disgraceful, infamous; a disreputable person.
s.m. Immaturity; indigestion
adj. (f. -ā), Unripe, uncooked, raw, immature, imperfect.
s.m. An indelible disgrace.
s.m. Hindrance; detriment, injury, mischief; wickedness.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Injurious, mischievous; an enemy, injurer, &c.
s.m. Mean or unworthy action; wickedness.
s.f. Disrepute, dishonour, disgrace, infamy. — apakīrtti karnā (-kī), To defame, disgrace, &c.
s.f. Unripeness, rawness, immaturity.
adj. (f. -ā), Bad, inferior, deteriorated.
s.f. = S اپکرشٿتو अपकृष्टत्व apakr̤ishṭatwa, s.m. Badness, vileness, inferiority, deterioration; baseness, lowness, meanness of rank.
s.m. = S اپکرشٿتا अपकृष्टता apakr̤ishṭatā, s.f. Badness, vileness, inferiority, deterioration; baseness, lowness, meanness of rank.
adj. (f. -ā), Without wings; without assistance or protection.
= H اپلچهن उपलच्छण upaloććhaṇ, = H اپلچهنا अपलच्छणा apalaććhaṇā, Corr. of the three following words, qq.v.
= H اپلچهن अपलच्छण apalaććhaṇ, = H اپلچهن उपलच्छण upaloććhaṇ, Corr. of the three following words, qq.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Shameless, immodest, impudent; — shameless or immodest person.
s.f. Shamelessness, impudence.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), = اپلج apalajja, q.v.
s.m. A bad omen, bad sign.
adj. (f. -ī), Of bad omen, ominous; unlucky; of suspicious appearance.
s.m. Disrespect, disparagement, disgrace, dishonour; affront; contempt. — apamān karnā (-kā), To show disrespect (to); to despise, disgrace, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Dishonouring; despising; disgraced, disreputable, dishonourable; — a disreputable person, &c.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Disrespected, degraded, disgraced.
adj. (f. -ā), Disreputable, dishonourable.
= اپنپا apanpā, q.v.
s.f. Sudden or untimely death; death from some accident, and not from sickness or decay.
s.m. The anus; ventris crepitus.
= H اپانا अपाना apānā pron. m. = اپنا apnā, q.v.
= H آپن त्रापन, आपुन āpan and āpun, (prov.) = اپنا apnā, q.v.
= H اپن अपन, अप्पन, apan and appan, (prov.) = اپنا apnā, q.v.
= H اپان अपान apān pron. m. = اپنا apnā, q.v.
s.m. The outer corner of the eye; a sectarial mark on the forehead; a cripple
subs: (f. -ā), Crippled. — apāng-darśan, s.m. A side glance, a leer.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Infirm (cf. apāng).
s.m. An emerald.
s.f. Reproach; abuse; blasphemy.
s.m. (f. -ikā), abuser; blasphemer.
part. adj. (f. -ā) & s.m. Coming to another for shelter; afflicted; obtained, acquired, gained; — one who comes to another for shelter, a refugee.

ety: apan, q.v.+pā = S. aff. त्व twa
def: One's self (used only in the acc. e.g. — ) apaṅpā baćānā, to guard or protect oneself.
s.m. Wrong road; bad road; devious track; heresy.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Having lost one's way; devious, going astray; heretical; — one who has lost his way, wanderer; heretic.
adj. (f. -ā), Sinless, innocent, guiltless.
For compounds having āp for the first member, v.s.v. آپ āp.
adj. Shoreless; unbounded, boundless, interminable, infinite; excessive; impassable; unsurpassed; unrivalled.
adj. (f. -ā), Another, other, a second; following; inferior; posterior; opposite; western. — aparaṅća, adv. Moreover.

s.m. Fault, crime, transgression, guilt, offence; injury, wrong, violence. — aparādh karnā (-kā), To commit an offence (against); to sin (against). — aparādh lagānā (-par, -ko), To impute a crime (to), to accuse, charge; to defame, slauder.
adj. (f. -ikā), Pertaining to guilt.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Guilty (of, -kā); faulty, offending; — offender, criminal, sinner.
s.m. The third watch of the day; the afternoon.
s.m. Non-defeat; victory; invincibleness.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Not defeated; invincible; unerring.
s.f. The name of a twining shrub, Clitoria ternatea (called also koyal and kawwā-ṭhenṭī, 'crow's-beak').
adj. (f. -ā), Boundless, infinite.
adv. Moreover, besides, further, also. (See apara.)
= H اپڙهے अपढ़े apaṛhe, = انپڙهہ anpaṛh, q.v.
= H اپڙهہ अपढ़ apaṛh, = انپڙهہ anpaṛh, q.v.
s.f. = اپريکشا aparīkshā, q.v.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Unconversant or unacquainted (with, -se); unknowable, unrecognizable; unappreciable, intangible, indefinite.
s.f. Want of trial or proof, an insufficient trial of anything.
part. adj. (f. -ā), Unmeasured, unlimited.
s.f. Deviation from established custom; bad course.

r: ā-paṛnā, a-paṛnā, ān-paṛnā, ānā, paṛnā, par
def: (also a-paṛnā, and ān-paṛnā, from ānā and paṛnā, qq.v.), To come to pass, come to hand; to come unexpectedly or suddenly; to fall suddenly (upon, -par).
s.m. The state of a Hindū after bathing previous to worship or to eating, during which it is unlawful for him to touch any one.
s.f. Kindred, brotherhood, fraternity; fellowship
subs: each other, one another. — āpas-dārī (see dārī), s.f. Kindred, &c. = āpas

and āpas-kā, adj. (f. -ī), Private, family (e.g. āpas-kā muʻāmlā, 'a private, or family, affair,' or 'an affair concerning our- or themselves'). — āpas-meṅ, adv. Among themselves, with each other or one another, together, mutually, in common, in concert
To live together; to live together as man and wife, to live in adultery; to live apart from men and in unnatural intercourse with one another (as certain women in India do).

def: s.m. Wind broken backwards; a disagreeable sound; ungrammatical language; an unmeaning or jingling word appended to another (e.g. wānī, in pānī wānī: — syn. تابع tābiʻ).
